profile.archetype: Deep-Tech Analytical Engineer / Future Technical Leader
profile.mission: Develop high-impact engineering systems across space, energy, and health domains
superpowers: Strong analytical rigor in complex systems
Deep understanding of error propagation and system reliability
Ability to model, simulate, and validate real-world engineering systems
Operates effectively in high-precision, high-stakes environments

strengths: system error budgeting and reliability engineering
control, dynamics, and kinematics of complex systems
validation and verification of deployed systems
weaknesses: limited exposure to product definition and market-driven development
less experience with rapid prototyping or iterative MVP-style building
focus tends toward optimization over exploration

builder_style.preference: well-defined problems with clear physical constraints

unfair_advantages: experience in both space and nuclear sectors
rare combination of theory + hands-on validation
ability to operate at subsystem level with deep understanding
early leadership in technically complex environments
constraints: limited exposure to commercial and customer-facing aspects
career focused on technical depth over breadth
not yet positioned as full system owner or product driver

characteristics: complex systems with high reliability requirements
deep technical challenges over fast iteration

anti_patterns: low-rigor environments with weak engineering standards
purely hype-driven or shallow tech projects
products disconnected from real physical constraints
gaps_to_close: understanding of market dynamics and product strategy
exposure to customer needs and business context
experience defining problems (not just solving them)
ownership of systems end-to-end (beyond analysis scope)

likes: systems with measurable performance and reliability

profile.long_term_potential: Technical leader in deep-tech industries (space, energy, robotics), with potential to evolve into CTO-level roles if market and product exposure are developed
